title: Cells, Bulbs and Buzzers Circuit Lab

effectTimelines.label: Bulb glow
effectTimelines.description: Bulb brightness changes as more cells are added to the complete circuit.

effectTimelines.label: Buzzer pulse
effectTimelines.description: The buzzer vibrates harder and the sound rings expand further as the cell count increases.

steps.title: Define the circuit question
steps.prompt: How does the number of cells in a complete circuit affect the output device?
steps.guidance: Choose whether you want to focus on the bulb or the buzzer.

options.label: How does the number of cells affect bulb brightness?
How does the number of cells affect buzzer volume?

steps.title: Plan variables and controls
steps.prompt: Identify what you will change and what you will keep the same in the complete circuit.
steps.guidance: A fair test changes one thing and keeps the rest of the circuit setup steady.

options.label: Keep the circuit complete each time

steps.title: Predict the circuit outcome
steps.prompt: Predict how the bulb brightness or buzzer volume will change when more cells are added.
steps.guidance: Use the setup preview to think about energy moving around the complete circuit.
steps.sentenceStem: I predict the output will ___ because ___.

steps.title: Run the complete circuit
steps.prompt: Choose the number of cells and the output device, then close the complete circuit.
steps.guidance: Watch the full wire loop, then focus on the bulb glow or buzzer rings.

steps.title: Record what changed
steps.prompt: Record how the bulb brightness or buzzer volume changed when the number of cells changed.
steps.guidance: Use the full circuit scene and the trial table as evidence.

steps.title: Analyse the pattern
steps.prompt: What pattern do you notice between the number of cells and the output strength?

steps.title: Conclude from the evidence
steps.prompt: Use your observations to explain how changing the number of cells affected the output device.
steps.guidance: Refer to the complete circuit and at least one recorded result.

steps.title: Evaluate the model
steps.prompt: How could this circuit test be improved if you were using real classroom equipment?
steps.guidance: Think about repeats, accurate observation and checking the circuit each time.

teacherNotes: The scene shows a complete circuit all the way round so pupils can talk about current needing a closed loop.
Use the bulb and buzzer as two familiar KS2 outputs rather than introducing resistance.
safetyNote: Real electrical investigations should use low-voltage classroom equipment and teacher supervision.
misconceptionChecks: The circuit must be complete for the bulb or buzzer to work.
Adding cells can increase the output without changing the wires or output device.

hero.blurb: Build a complete circuit, change the number of cells and see the bulb glow or buzzer rings respond immediately.
